programme.title: Conclave Begins
programme.desc: Special coverage from Rome as the conclave to pick the next pope begins.

programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.title: CTV National News with Omar Sachedina
programme.sub-title: CTV National News for Tuesday, May 6, 2025
programme.desc: CTV National News with Omar Sachedina is Canada's #1 national newscast

programme.title: CTV News: Across Canada
programme.desc: Top stories from coast-to-coast as reported by CTV News local journalists across Canada.

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: A boy's disappearance leads to a community turning on his parents.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Chicken gyro dupe; repurposing household items as organizers; Japandi décor trend with Colin and Justin; Arkells' frontman Max Kerman.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Bacon and pea gnocchi; food and drink crowd-pleasers; must-have gardening gadgets; outdoor pet safety; "North of North" star Anna Lambe.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Mary and her guests make homemade dupes of store-bought classics, like chocolate freezer cake and mac 'n cheese.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Sheet-pan salmon; cluttered closet transformation and organizing products; 'Sullivan's Crossing's' Morgan Kohan and Chad Michael Murray.

programme.title: The Good Stuff with Mary Berg
programme.desc: Viewers who've overcome challenges get makeovers; 2nd day hair hacks; adding pops of colour to your look; learning how to make a hand roll.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary is all about cooking smarter, not harder. She shares no-sweat supermarket shortcuts to save you from kitchen time and stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Save your abundance of fresh herbs from wilting away with Mary’s recipes to use ‘em up in a jiff.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the season with Mary’s recipes that take advantage of limited-edition produce, including tomatoes, zucchini, peppers and berries.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re a newbie baker or a pro who wants to take it easy, these recipes are all about supermarket hacks to raise your baking game.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Make your own checkered tablecloth magic with Mary’s recipes for Italian-American restaurant night.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Hosted by chef and two-time Canadian Screen Award-winner Mary Berg, follows Berg as she takes viewers step-by-step through recipes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Impress pals with recipes that are great to make and easier to take, and they just might steal the show at the next potluck.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows how to up the baking game without breaking the bank on fancy gadgets. There’s no specialty equipment here!

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary keeps shopping lists short with flavourful dishes made up of just a few ingredients.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the veg life with Mary’s meatless recipes that are so much more than just a bowl of greens.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Japanese Recipes Inspired by Western Cuisine
programme.desc: Chef and cookbook author Hana Etsuko Dethlefsen makes recipes that are inspired by western cuisine

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef Natalia Machado knows that land well, and she's making recipes that come from Patagonia.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Chef and cookbook author Hana Etsuko Dethlefsen introduces us to some of the most popular noodles in Japanese cuisine.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Thai chef and cookbook author Pailin Chongchitnant takes us on a culinary trip of Chiang Mai.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hana teaches us the secrets behind forming the delicate tofu pockets, and why the pockets are named after the Shinto god Inari.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Thai cookbook author and chef Pailin Chongchitnant will show you how to make curry at home from scratch.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Japanese Food Inspired by Other Cultures
programme.desc: Chef Hana makes chicken wings, Japanese curry and rice and chawanmushi - a savoury creamy custard.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Surprisingly, Thais don't grill much at home, although they love to buy grilled food from street vendors.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Thai Food Inspired by Other Cultures
programme.desc: Thai chef Pailin Chongchitnant shows us some Thai dishes that were inspired by food from other cultures.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s all about street food in this Vietnamese episode.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang cooks up Cantonese recipes perfect for days when you need comfort food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Fun host Lisa Nguyen shows us just how versatile noodles are.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa Nguyen shows us recipes from Vietnam's capital city of Hanoi.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Home cook Mary Tang shows us popular Hong Kong culinary delights.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us how to celebrate the Moon Festival.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang cooks up traditional Cantonese dim sum recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us four easy Cantonese seafood recipes.

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: In our search for life beyond Earth, seeking water on other planetary bodies is an important first step.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope will be our key to understanding where we fit in our enormous universe.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Time and space converge in black holes. Now we can peer into its abyss to discover its secrets.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Earth's new generation of orbiting telescopes promises to expand our universe of knowledge about our origins.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: Scientists are exploring planets and moons that may once have supported life. Saturn's moon, Europa, is one.

programme.title: Planet Rethink
programme.desc: Pollution is everywhere. But all is not dire. We need to increase resource efficiency to preserve our planet.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: Solar Superstorms: Journey to the Center of the Sun
programme.desc: As technology and electrical grids have spread, big solar storms pose a threat equal to the worst hurricanes.

programme.title: Day of the Asteroid
programme.desc: Armed with new defensive technologies, scientists are getting ready for the Day of the Asteroid.

programme.title: Exoplanets: Thousands of New Worlds
programme.desc: Are we alone? Scientists challenge traditional planetary science to seek and observe worlds called exoplanets.

programme.title: Earth's Survival: Decoding the Science
programme.desc: This documentary explains how we may be in the middle of the most crucial moment of Earth’s history.

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Expedition Antarctica
programme.desc: A hardy research crew seeks clues about the fate of our planet beneath the icy waters of the Antarctic Ocean.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Mars Calling: Manifest Destiny or Grand Illusion
programme.desc: This film merges the mythic and the scientific to show that Mars may not be what we imagine.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: We're looking hard, but the numbers aren't stacking up. Is life on other planets really that hard to find?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Two new space probes can now orbit closer to the Sun than ever before, enabling groundbreaking research.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Scientists are studying the benefits of a hybrid moon mission, one where humans and robots work in tandem.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Climate change is causing increasing havoc to our environment. How can science respond to this challenge?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Venus may be considered Earth's evil twin. Hostile and unforgiving, Venus is the subject of intense scrutiny.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The International Space Station is now the only manned station in space, but soon there may be more.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Asteroids are everywhere in space. We count them, examine why they exist, and track them for Earth's safety.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.desc: About 180 years after Darwin, his great-granddaughter follows his path, traveling from Tenerife to the Amazon.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Earthquakes baffle experts as they try to gather the information necessary to predict the next big one.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Melting ice at Earth's poles: Do we still have a chance to stop it from melting and prevent its repercussions?

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an exciting journey of discovery into an almost untouched wilderness: The Jura Mountains.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: This episode focuses on Ticino, reaching from the Tessin Alps down to the charming Muggio Valley.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Take an amazing journey from the highest peaks of the Bernese Alps to a spectacular finish at the Rhine Falls.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The ibex, marmot, and European adder have all developed strategies to survive the harsh alpine winter.

programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Cuba to Go
programme.desc: Ever since the embargo was lifted in the summer of 2015, Cuba’s popularity has been steadily increasing.

programme.title: Cuba to Go
programme.desc: Ever since the embargo was lifted in the summer of 2015, Cuba’s popularity has been steadily increasing.

programme.title: mareTV: Martinique
programme.desc: The island of Martinique is a piece of France in the Caribbean, between Florida and South America. And of course, Savoir-vivre applies just as it does on the other side of the Atlantic, between the English Channel and the Mediterranean. Jacky Pascault is the first and only man to cultivate mushrooms in the Caribbean. Everywhere else, they grow on artificially fertilised soil, but Jacky's Champignons Tropicaux flourish on sugar cane fibres. Nicole Vilo tailors hats and dresses out of coconut palms. Of course, the catwalk for the models wearing her first collection is one of the countless dream beaches. The Martiniquais sail on boats they call Yole, which have no keel and are therefore difficult to navigate. The crew members hang over the water in breakneck fashion on long wooden poles. Just the job for Yole-boss Max' "problem kids": they not only learn complicated handling, but also team spirit.

programme.title: mareTV: Curacao, Blue Wonder of the Caribbean
programme.desc: Curacao is one of the most popular attractions for countless cruise ships on their trips through the Caribbean, because it is located outside of the hurricane belt. This is why Eric Miguel is constantly in action. He is the bridge keeper of the Queen Emma Bridge, the landmark of Curacao's capital, Willemstad. After his shift, Eric goes to Yvonne Troeman's cook-shop. Her speciality is Kadushi, a cactus soup. The ingredients include cactus flesh, pigtail's, mussels and a few secret "treats". Barbara van Bebber is the only submarine pilot on Curacao and literally gets to the bottom of things amidst the turquoise-blue wonder of the Caribbean. She charters out her sub to marine biologists and wealthy private explorers, simultaneously cleaning up on the seabed. Her "Curasub" has claw arms, which she employs to remove anything that doesn't belong down there: beer bottles, car tyres, and plastic refuse. Jeroen Eikelenboom simply carts sand from a neighbouring cove to his own surf club in his favourite bay.

programme.title: mareTV: Mexico's Caribbean Coast
programme.desc: The palm tree laden beaches on Mexico's Riviera Maya on the Yucatan peninsula are endless, and the turquoise sea with its gentle Caribbean surf is a warm 25 degrees during the mild winter in this part of the world. Between Cancún and Punta Allen, where once there was little more than bushland and a few sleepy fishing villages, vibrant life has evolved during the past three decades. Richard Sowa has lived on a floating island for the past 15 years: an island he built himself out of empty plastic bottles. The bon vivant him-self sees it as a "solution for our planet's refuse problem". Off Punta Allen, a village in the bay of Sian Ka'an, Mexican Mara Coral Ley and her cousin Kim, continue to catch lobsters by adhering to the traditional method and were awarded an "Eco seal for sustainable fishing" for their trouble. It is silent under water off Isla Mujeres and it is here that the artist Jason deCaires has constructed a sculpture park. Hundreds of lifesize figures form an artificial reef at a depth of 12 metres. Fish and plants complete his work of art. The master himself is convinced: "Mother Nature is the true artist".

programme.title: Caribbean sisters: The Lesser Antilles St. Kitts & Nevis
programme.desc: In the centre of the Caribbean, volcanoes rise steeply from the sea, thus shaping the sister islands of St. Kitts & Nevis. Their slopes, covered in lush rainforests, snow-white, sandy beaches and the surrounding tur-quoise tinted waters provide the ultimate setting for this record-breaking, albeit tiny island paradise: Both islets are the smallest of the Caribbean dwarf states and also rank among the world’s ten smallest states. A hot pepper sauce, made of a creative mixture of ginger, garlic, thyme and extraordinarily hot chillies that thrive in the hot and humid climate of Nevis, is simmering on the stove of Violet and Llewellyn’s house. Lemmy Pemberton digs up abandoned turtle nests with his bare hands, as he wants to be certain that all of the babies have indeed managed to make it. Members of the Turtle Group catch sea turtles in order to take their measurements and fit them with transmitters. Taxi bus-es are not only the main means of transport on St. Kitts & Nevis, they are also an artistic expression of each driver’s personality. Colourfully embellished, each taxi represents island art on wheels.

programme.title: Cuba - Brilliantly Caribbean
programme.desc: Even anyone who has not yet been to Cuba can conjure up certain images of American vintage street cruisers, imposing buildings that have seen better days, white sandy beaches full of palms and loving couples on the Malecón against a backdrop of crashing waves. This island is somewhat of a myth and its people generate a special joie de vivre. The legendary Hershey Train departs for Matanzas from the capital, Havana. On the oldest electrified railway line in the Caribbean, 60-year-old Spanish carriages make their way along al-most 100 kilometres of coastline in the most relaxed way possible. The Salvavidas on Varadero, probably the most famous beach on the island, have a particular-ly beautiful workplace. The lifeguards' territory ex-tends for more than 20 kilometres. In order to protect the coasts of Cuba, mangroves have been replanted here for many years. The project is supported by the UN and planting is often carried out by schoolchildren. In doing so, Cuban children learn how to treat their maritime habitat in a sustainable fashion. The former colonial city of Trinidad on Cuba's south coast is gen-erally considered to be one of the most beautiful places in the entire Caribbean. The houses here are particular-ly colourful. A plethora of magnificent buildings can be seen at the Plaza Mayor, the neo-baroque main square.

programme.title: mareTV: The Caribbean for explorers: St. Vincent and the Grenadines
programme.desc: Much more Caribbean than on the islands of St. Vincent and the Grenadines is hardly possible. Deserted beaches, uninhabited islands, lush vegetation, the 33 islands in total are dreamlike with extremely hospitable inhabitants. Miranda Phillips runs a laundry and water boat on Bequia. Every morning, the 32-year-old navigates from yacht to yacht with her small bright green service vehicle and is already expected by most skippers. Miranda promises radiantly fresh laundry, which she delivers again the same day. And so there is always something going on in her tiny laundrette in the harbour. It turns into a karaoke bar in the evening, because that is Miranda's great passion. Eban Olliver has many jobs at once. He is the lighthouse keeper of Kingstown. And that means a lot of responsibility in St. Vincent's biggest port city. Because Eban doesn't just change the light bulbs, he's a pilot dispatcher, harbour master and guard all in one person. First contact with ships is particularly important. Once he even saved the entire nation from an Ebola virus.

programme.title: mareTV: Peru - Coast of the Incas
programme.desc: Dramatically, the coast plunges into the sea, the so-called Costa of Peru is a gigantic desert on the Pacific Ocean, 2,400 kilometers long. Rugged, bizarre, like from another star. Ancient cultures settled here, the Moche and the Chimú. Later the proud Inca, the self-proclaimed royal people of the Andes, ruled. In the small town of Huanchaco in northern Peru, the fishermen build a new boat every month: from reed. The small ships are called Caballito de Totora (reed horses), because the shape is reminiscent of a horse and the fishermen ride in it kneeling in the waves. About 2,000 years ago, the moche used it to go out to sea. Carlos Segura Azola is a descendant of the moche. He is currently building a "reed horse" again. But actually something completely different occupies him. Carlos has been afraid of the sea ever since his father drowned at work. The fisherman consults a Brucha, a witch. She tries to drive him out of panic at the Pacific with mysterious ceremonies. At some point Carlos' reed boat is finished and he has to get out to his nets again. In the south, in the port city of Pisco, there are hardly any private cars. Mototaxis on three wheels are the means of transport here. Every moto looks different, because the young drivers compete with each other. They constantly pimp out their tricycles with spoilers, mirrors and all kinds of decorations. Because if you stand out in the traffic jam, you get the most passengers. But nowhere else in Peru is road traffic as dense and chaotic as in the capital Lima. That's why Juan Ortiz goes daily with his Delta glider over the coastal road into the air. He is the flying reporter and reports live for the traffic news. And he is not alone with his aircraft. Dozens of paragliders float along the cliffs of Lima at any time of the day. The Pacific provides a constant upwind on the up to 160 meter high cliffs. Somewhere in the labyrinth of the metropolis of millions hundreds of believers trot to the beat of the music of a Jesus statue on the cross. A procession for the "Lord of Miracles", the brass band intones church songs. The music student Bruno Alvarado drags and plays the largest instrument, a sousaphone. Every day there are dozens of processions in Lima. Practical for Bruno, he finances his studies with the musical accompaniment. Away from the hustle and bustle of the big city, in the Paracas National Park, it is deserted. Luis Zapato runs a kiosk on a lonely beach amidst huge sand dunes. The kiosk has meanwhile developed into a gourmet temple, even the capital city dwellers flock here. Especially because of the scallops that Luis is the only licence holder to collect far and wide from the seabed. Inti Mar he called his beach snack, Inti is the sun god, Mar the sea. At Luis you literally eat like a sun god at the sea. This is only possible here, on the Inca coast.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, The City and the Sea
programme.desc: The journey up the coast continues through Delaware to suburban New Jersey, and onwards into the heart of Manhattan. In Delaware, famous as a corporate tax haven, we visit a winery. In New York City we zip around with a bicycle courier and then head out to Long Island for oysters.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, Florida - The Sunshine State
programme.desc: Nowhere is the American way of life as relaxed as on Florida's beaches. The Keys, a chain of islands at the far southern end of the state, reveal a more laid-back side of the USA. But all of Florida amazes -- with secluded beaches, unspoiled nature, and the legacy of the early European settlers.

programme.title: The U.S. East Coast
programme.sub-title: The U.S. East Coast, The Heart of New England
programme.desc: From New York, the journey up the coast leads into the heart of New England. We visit Yale; spend some time in the 19th century mansions at Newport, RI; tour the coastal fishing and vacation towns of Massachusetts, and then meet a modern entrepreneur in Boston and see her rooftop gardens.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: It's a water world and life couldn't survive without it. And thanks to it, a mind blowing diversity of creatures thrive beneath the surface. Born to enjoy the riches underwater they must master their body's adaptations, learn to find food, to escape danger and above all become expert swimmers! Whether it's in rivers, on beaches or in the deep ocean, all must overcome the obstacles and fulfil their destiny, all are born to swim! Of all the habitats on earth, the ocean is perhaps the most challenging. Here salt, temperatures, currents and predators can make life difficult; it's not an obvious place to want to bring up your babies… But even in these waters generation after generation beats the odds. They have overcome the challenges and become some of the most beautiful and graceful of creatures. But any baby born to swim has a lot to learn… All over the world there are creatures born to swim, and though humans are not, we seem determined to join them. Perhaps we are envious of their grace. Maybe we are all water babies at heart

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 1: Against all Odds
programme.desc: The two sisters are born as white as snow in May 2009 in South Africa’s Kruger Park. Growing up in this vast, wild wilderness they must not only overcome the same survival challenges that all young lion cubs face, they must also overcome the threats their high visibility brings. In the African bush, anything unusual stands out and is often eliminated by eagle-eyed predators. Will our cubs survive? The odds are stacked against them – 80 percent of all newborn lion cubs don’t make it to adulthood.

programme.title: White Lions: Born Wild
programme.sub-title: White Lions: Born Wild - Episode 2: Fight for Survival
programme.desc: ‘White Lions – Born Wild’ is a true wildlife story, with strong characters and closely studied behaviour. We follow the cubs’ progress as they embark on the pathway to adulthood, learning to hunt, learning to avoid danger, learning to live in their white coats. This is a strong behaviour-led film, a coming-of-age story of a very rare and unusual pair of lions. Through their eyes, we learn what it is really like to be a lion in Africa.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Flying Foxes and Heat Waves
programme.desc: While the Tolga Bat Hospital is looking after endangered flying foxes, researchers are using thermographic cameras to search for flying foxes and koalas. But they can also detect bush and wildfire more quickly.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Sea Turtles and Coral Reefs
programme.desc: The Cairns Turtle Rehabilitation Center (CTRC) raises and cares for sick, or injured turtles, while the Schmidt Ocean Institute goes on a diving mission with a high-tech underwater rover. In this way, we learn firsthand about the state of Australia's sensitive marine animals' natural habitat and what needs to be done to ensure their survival.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.desc: Tasmania's Bonorong Wildlife Sanctuary used to be a traditional wildlife park - now it is an innovative and active wildlife sanctuary and home to the famous Tasmanian Devil, to wombats, and many other native species. But unfortunately, "Tassie Devils" are particularly endangered by the Devil Facial Tumor Disease (DFTD). We accompany the team of young, ambitious scientists on their fight against the disease.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.desc: Lone Pine Koala Sanctuary was opened in 1927 at a time when Koalas were still being killed on a large scale for the fur trade. Today, hardly anything threatens the natural habitats of these animals as much as the desertification of the forests. We let the drones fly and are there when a new tree is planted.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.desc: Visiting the fenced nature reserve Arid Recovery in southern Australia, we witness the care of an injured kangaroo and follow researchers at the Hidden Vale Wildlife Centre near Brisbane as they train native animals to detect tracks of threatening predators and then move to safety.

programme.title: Wildlife Diaries Australia
programme.sub-title: Of Fairy Penguins and GPS Trackers
programme.desc: Australia's largest fur seal colony is located on Phillip Island. Night after night, a breathtaking spectacle takes place here: At sunset, countless fairy penguins return from their hunt for fish. In the station near the penguin colony, pioneering scientific research is conducted around the cute animals. We learn how dedicated scientists are trying to secure the future of the fairy penguins with the help of GPS trackers.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife film-makers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In Alaska a cunning grizzly threatens the son of Andreas Kieling, on the remote Wrangel Island in northern-east Siberia his colleague Uwe Anders is taken by surprise by a a polar bear, and off the coast of South Africa a diving-team is unexpectedly surrounded by white sharks. But the life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, life-threatening diseases and failing technical equipment: Deep in the vast rainforests of West Africa cameraman Klaus Scheurich nearly dies of malaria, and in South India a film team narrowly escapes death in a hot-air balloon accident. But they also meet remarkable people such as a zoo keeper in Cameroon who feels at ease playing with half-grown and adult gorillas - the most powerful of all great apes. The first episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. A cunning grizzly grabs a salmon off Andreas Kieling's fishing rod, in the depths of the Amazon River a diving team has a spooky encounter with piranhas, in the Mediterranean Sea the currents nearly push Thomas Behrend into the mouths of angry sperm whales defending a newborn - and cameramen who want to film jaguars in the dense jungle of South America become easy prey for the well camouflaged cats. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces and failing technical equipment: In the swamps of North Australia violent storms hold up the crossing of tearing rivers, a volcano in the South Seas spits burning lumps of lava into the base camp, and in Siberia a camera team breaks through the ice and has to camp at minus 30°C in the middle of nowhere. The second episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In the Kruger National Park Josef Jozefowicz steps into a lionesses' den to film her cubs, in Alaska Andreas Kieling mingles with giant elk bulls in order to film them in action, and in Florida his colleague Zoltan Török gets very close to an alligator mother guarding its nest. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, and failing technical equipment: the strong and tearing current of the Yukon River throws Andreas Kieling's boat onto a gravel bank, in the Democratic Republik of Congo camerawoman Heike Grebe is separated from her colleagues in the dense rainforest, in Kamchatka a film team only just escapes a gigantic landslide, and in Finland a hot-air balloon catches fire and becomes the nightmare of Oliver Goetzl and Ivo Nörenberg. The last episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: The Cape of Wild Animals, Episode 5
programme.desc: The movie focuses on committed animal rights activists, who fight for the lives of their charges every day, in diverse protection projects and rescue centres along the South African Garden Route.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: Baby animals beguile with their innocent charm. But while some remain harmless, others have a deep-rooted instinct. It may take weeks or months of training to hone their skills. Their landscapes and lifestyles maybe very different, but these babies are born to hunt.

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: In a land of dangers, plant-eating animals are always at risk, and never more so than at the time of their birth; out on the open they are vulnerable and there are few places to hide. But luckily some are born to run …

programme.title: The Original Whale Riders
programme.desc: A scientist studies whale parasites to protect them, but contact is risky due to their size and force.

programme.title: River Jaws
programme.desc: Since 1950, Europe's largest freshwater fish, the catfish, has harmed pikes, pigeons, and bathers.

programme.title: Searching For The Coast Wolves
programme.desc: Shot in the rain forests of Canada's west coast, this documentary records the life of the rare coastal wolves.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In Kenya's Masai Mara, olive baboons endure floods, rivalries, and attacks, showcasing survival.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: At Murchison Falls, monitor lizards raid Nile crocodile nests, while crocs seek revenge.

programme.title: Giant Sea Serpent, Meet The Myth
programme.desc: Scientific boys off French Riviera uncover secrets of the giant oarfish, likely the sea serpent myth's source.

programme.title: Wild Morocco Monkeys And Snakes
programme.desc: Documentary highlights Morocco's unique wildlife, featuring Barbary apes & rare reptiles in their habitats.

programme.title: Ibex: Acrobats On The Rocks
programme.desc: The film follows Buck, an Alpine Ibex from Italy's Gran Paradiso, highlighting his life and majestic beauty.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson, animal lover, faces the challenge of relocating 28 lions and 14 hyenas.

programme.title: The Tick and the Bird
programme.desc: A flea in a Laysan Albatross' feathers crosses the Pacific, landing on Midway Island to mate & form a family.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: The water beetle emerges in spring, battling for survival in a pond with various creatures.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In French Guyana's Amazon, a young jaguar faces fierce warrior ants, revealing jungle drama.

programme.title: The Original Whale Riders
programme.desc: A scientist studies whale parasites to protect them, but contact is risky due to their size and force.

programme.title: River Jaws
programme.desc: Since 1950, Europe's largest freshwater fish, the catfish, has harmed pikes, pigeons, and bathers.

programme.title: Searching For The Coast Wolves
programme.desc: Shot in the rain forests of Canada's west coast, this documentary records the life of the rare coastal wolves.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: In Kenya's Masai Mara, olive baboons endure floods, rivalries, and attacks, showcasing survival.

programme.title: Conflicts of Nature
programme.desc: At Murchison Falls, monitor lizards raid Nile crocodile nests, while crocs seek revenge.

programme.title: Giant Sea Serpent, Meet The Myth
programme.desc: Scientific boys off French Riviera uncover secrets of the giant oarfish, likely the sea serpent myth's source.

programme.title: Wild Morocco Monkeys And Snakes
programme.desc: Documentary highlights Morocco's unique wildlife, featuring Barbary apes & rare reptiles in their habitats.

programme.title: Ibex: Acrobats On The Rocks
programme.desc: The film follows Buck, an Alpine Ibex from Italy's Gran Paradiso, highlighting his life and majestic beauty.

programme.title: The Tick and the Bird
programme.desc: A flea in a Laysan Albatross' feathers crosses the Pacific, landing on Midway Island to mate & form a family.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Mutualism explores animal cooperation for survival, turning host-parasite relationships into alliances.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Playing games helps children and animals develop coordination, adaptability, and social skills.

programme.title: Sperm Whales: Dealing with the Unexpected
programme.desc: The Sperm Whale family in the Crozet Islands faces threats from humans, fishing lines, and deep-sea monsters.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands our understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The Breakdown
programme.desc: Make sense of your world with original reporting that breaks down the world's biggest news stories.

programme.title: Newfoundland & Labrador Now
programme.desc: News, investigations and community stories in NL and beyond.

programme.title: Newfoundland & Labrador Now
programme.desc: News, investigations and community stories in NL and beyond.

programme.title: Newfoundland & Labrador Now
programme.desc: News, investigations and community stories in NL and beyond.

programme.desc: News, investigations and community stories in NL and beyond.

programme.title: Newfoundland & Labrador Now
programme.desc: News, investigations and community stories in NL and beyond.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands our understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The Breakdown
programme.desc: Make sense of your world with original reporting that breaks down the world's biggest news stories.

programme.title: CBC News Here and Now
programme.desc: Carolyn Stokes and Ashley Brauweiler deliver a complete package of local news and weather.

programme.title: Weather AM
programme.desc: Get your local forecast and weather headlines from right here in Canada, and around the globe.

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les disappears into the forested Transylvanian Alps to test the skills of Romania's top Search and Rescue Team

programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: Your Weather Today
programme.desc: National and local forecast details for across the country

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Your Weather Today
programme.desc: National and local forecast details for across the country

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Your Weather Today

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Your Weather Today
programme.desc: National and local forecast details for across the country

programme.title: Destination Wild
programme.desc: Nestled alongside Canada's south west coast, Vancouver Island is home to the richest Pacific wildlife.

programme.title: Destination Wild
programme.desc: Argentina's northern territory contains one of the largest reserves in the country. Iberá is a vast expanse of swamps, marshes and lagoons, with a biodiversity count to rival the Pantanal.

programme.title: Kanga Pan
programme.desc: Kanga Pan: An African Horror Story is a spine-chilling, one-off wildlife documentary. Who is the killer on the loose? Who is terrorising the pan? An investigation needs to be undertaken.

programme.title: Desert Land of the Middle East
programme.desc: The Middle East is also home to the Red Sea, one of the saltiest seas in the world, yet despite this, over ten per cent of this sea's fish species are found nowhere else on the planet.

programme.title: Wild Portugal
programme.desc: The richness of Portugal's coastal waters is amazing. Thousands of creatures live here, both in the water and on land. The portuguese coast is the ultimate frontier before the Atlantic Ocean. In the middle of the Atlantic, the archipelago of the Azores is home to one of the richest ocean ecosystems on the Planet. In this episode we will meet some of these species in a unique and revealing look at their lives.

programme.title: Destination Wild
programme.desc: Nestled alongside Canada's south west coast, Vancouver Island is home to the richest Pacific wildlife.

programme.title: Destination Wild
programme.desc: Argentina's northern territory contains one of the largest reserves in the country. Iberá is a vast expanse of swamps, marshes and lagoons, with a biodiversity count to rival the Pantanal.

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: How will the White House fall in a Life After People? Historical homes decay.

programme.title: Evolve
programme.desc: Sex: ultimate survival skill for life on Earth, from sharks to dinosaurs.

programme.title: Evolve
programme.desc: Life evolved from .02-micrometer bacteria to 110-foot blue whales.

programme.title: Evolve
programme.desc: Communication's impact on species' survival and evolution.

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: Destructive waters overtake the world we leave behind.

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: Man's world of food faces ruin supermarkets breed pests, some crops fail.

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: Toxins and chemicals ravage a world without people, turning waters acidic.

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: How will the White House fall in a Life After People? Historical homes decay.
